response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack and backend engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the resume shows no evidence of the core requirements for this role: no native Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no embedded firmware or LAN-switch development, no Linux kernel modification work, and no security-domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, with the bulk of recent work in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Fit for this embedded networking security position is very low.
strengths: 10+ years of professional software development across multiple domains and languages
Some prior C/C++ experience at National Instruments and Linux familiarity
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency indicated — a hard requirement is unmet
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or chip-vendor SDK experience
No demonstrated security-domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, tamper detection) or Linux kernel source modification
